Package org.eventb.core.sc.state

Interface ISymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>

All Known Subinterfaces:
IContextLabelSymbolTable, IEventLabelSymbolTable, IIdentifierSymbolTable, ILabelSymbolTable, IMachineLabelSymbolTable
All Known Implementing Classes:
ContextLabelSymbolTable, EventLabelSymbolTable, IdentifierSymbolTable, LabelSymbolTable, MachineLabelSymbolTable, StackedIdentifierSymbolTable, SymbolTable
public interface ISymbolTable<E extends IInternalElement,T extends IInternalElementType<? extends E>,I extends ISymbolInfo<E,T>>
Symbol table template used in various forms in the static checker.
Since:
1.1
See Also:
  • ILabelSymbolTable
  • IIdentifierSymbolTable

  • Method Summary

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    boolean
    containsKey(String symbol)
    Returns whether a symbol is contained in the symbol table.
    I
    getSymbolInfo(String symbol)
    Returns the symbol info for a symbol.
    void
    makeImmutable()
    Turns all symbols of the symbol table immutable
    void
    putSymbolInfo(I symbolInfo)
    Inserts a symbol info into the symbol table.
    boolean
    tryPutSymbolInfo(I symbolInfo)
    Attempts to insert a symbol info into the symbol table and tells if it succeeded.

  • Method Details

    • containsKey

      boolean containsKey(String symbol)
      Returns whether a symbol is contained in the symbol table.
      Parameters:
      symbol - the symbol to be looked up.
      Returns:
      true if the symbol is present, false otherwise

    • getSymbolInfo

      I getSymbolInfo(String symbol)
      Returns the symbol info for a symbol. If the symbol is not present null is returned. If this is stacked symbol table, it is searched from top to bottom.
      Parameters:
      symbol - the symbol corresponding to the symbol info
      Returns:
      the symbol info

    • putSymbolInfo

      void putSymbolInfo(I symbolInfo) throws org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException
      Inserts a symbol info into the symbol table. Multiple insertions are not allowed. The key of the corresponding symbol is symbolInfo.getSymbol(). If the symbol table is stacked, the symbol is inserted in the top level symbol table.

      This method should be used only when no exception should be raised. Otherwise, use the alternative method tryPutSymbolInfo(ISymbolInfo).

      Parameters:
      symbolInfo - the symbol info for the symbol
      Throws:
      org.eclipse.core.runtime.CoreException - if the symbol is already present in the symbol table
      See Also:
      • ISymbolInfo.getSymbol()

    • tryPutSymbolInfo

      boolean tryPutSymbolInfo(I symbolInfo)
      Attempts to insert a symbol info into the symbol table and tells if it succeeded. Multiple insertions are not allowed. The key of the corresponding symbol is symbolInfo.getSymbol(). If the symbol table is stacked, the symbol is inserted in the top level symbol table.

      This is similar to putSymbolInfo(ISymbolInfo) but returning a Boolean instead of throwing an exception.

      Parameters:
      symbolInfo - the symbol info for the symbol
      Returns:
      true if the symbol has been added to the table, false in case of collision
      Since:
      3.1
      See Also:
      • ISymbolInfo.getSymbol()

    • makeImmutable

      void makeImmutable()
      Turns all symbols of the symbol table immutable
